Petition For Order Determing Entitlement To Trust Property.
Off Calendar Apr-21-2015 Continued To May-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Antonchuk for (petitioner) John Martin-Rutherford.

The Court stated that this is a lost trust, there is only the drafting attorney's declaration but nothing to support it, that the trustee recorded a document 9 years after the death of Mr. Urban stating he was the successor trustee, that it has now been 24 years since Mr. Urban died and asked counsel if a notice of irrevocability, 16061.7(b)(2) had been served letting the potential heirs know that the trust had become irrevocable. Mr. Antonchuk stated he does not know, that the trustee does not remember serving such notice, that the trustee recalls distributing certain gifts to family members but there are no written records of such, that he recalls the father of the decedent received the life insurance benefits and that gifts were distributed to his sisters who lived in the mid-west but cannot be located. Mr. Antonchuk stated that the beneficiary had been treating the property as his own, that a recent need to re-finance the property brought up the issues of conveyance and suggests that an order from the court appointing Mr. Custer as trustee and the beneficiary entitled to 60% may suffice. The court stated that there is no evidentiary record to rely on for the terms of the trust other than the trustee's word. Mr. Antonchuk stated that we have the deeds transferring the property to the trust, the trustee who drafted the trust but understands the courts concern and will file a declaration regarding what steps have been taken to search for/locate the beneficiaries. The petition for order determining entitlement to trust property was continued to May 5, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
